decks.notes: The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's plans through counterspells and removal spells, while also maintaining card advantage through draw spells like Thoughtcast. The deck utilizes cheap creatures like Ornithopter and Faerie Seer to block and apply pressure, with the ability to bounce them back to hand with Ninja of the Deep Hours for value. The deck also has options for disruption with cards like Spell Pierce and Suffocating Fumes, creating a versatile control strategy that aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board while generating value through sacrificing creatures and recurring threats like Cauldron Familiar. It relies on a combination of removal spells like Cast Down and Crypt Rats, alongside recursion cards like Fanatical Offering and Witch's Cottage. The deck also includes defensive options like Troll of Khazad-dûm and lifegain from cards like Lembas. By utilizing the Golgari/Jund color combo, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent while maintaining a strong board presence with token generators like Khalni Garden.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive creatures combined with burn spells to deal damage quickly to the opponent. It also includes cards that provide card advantage and life gain to maintain pressure on the opponent. The mana base is constructed to ensure consistent access to both red and black mana sources. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature damage and direct burn spells, aiming to end the game as quickly as possible before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board by utilizing the Urzatron lands to generate massive amounts of mana. With cards like Myr Retriever and Pactdoll Terror, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a strong board presence through cards like Golem Foundry and Eviscerator's Insight. The inclusion of cards like Candy Trail and Weather the Storm helps to provide additional support and resilience against aggressive strategies. Ultimately, the goal of the deck is to outvalue the opponent and overwhelm them with powerful creatures and spells.

This deck focuses on controlling the board through efficient creatures and removal spells while utilizing gates to generate extra mana and card advantage. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by bouncing creatures for additional value and having a high density of low-cost artifacts to enable synergies. The main win condition is to overwhelm opponents with card advantage and efficient removal spells while using gates to accelerate mana development and outpace opponents in the mid to late game.

This deck focuses on maintaining a fast-paced offensive strategy by playing low-cost creatures and spells with direct damage potential. By continuously pressuring the opponent with creatures like Serrated Scorpion and Swarm, Being of Bees, as well as removal spells like Nasty End and Soul Reap, the deck aims to overwhelm and deplete the opponent's life total quickly. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Village Rites and Tyrant's Choice helps to maintain card advantage and further push the aggressive game plan. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and efficiency in executing its aggro strategy.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent alongside counterspells like Force Spike and Counterspell, the deck aims to control the board and protect its aggressive threats. With card draw and selection from Brainstorm, Consider, and Ponder, along with the ability to tap down opposing creatures with Tolarian Terror and Sleep of the Dead, the deck aims to maintain tempo and pressure throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of low-cost white creatures, known as weenies, and close out the game before the opponent has a chance to establish a strong defense. This deck utilizes efficient creatures like Squadron Hawk and Kor Skyfisher, along with token generators like Battle Screech, to go wide on the board and apply pressure early in the game. The deck also includes some removal options like Journey to Nowhere and Prismatic Strands to deal with threats from the opponent and maintain board control.

The main focus of this deck is to flood the board with cheap artifact creatures and use burn spells to clear the way for quick, aggressive attacks. Utilizing artifacts to reduce costs and generate card advantage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient offense while maintaining card advantage through card draw and recursion. With a low curve and a mix of creatures and burn spells, this deck aims to close out games quickly and decisively, putting pressure on the opponent from the very start.

This deck focuses on using control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating value through sacrificing creatures and drawing cards. By combining removal spells, board wipes, and recursion tools with creatures that provide additional benefits when they die, such as drawing cards or gaining life, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can establish a dominant board presence. Additionally, the inclusion of land cards like Khalni Garden and Bojuka Bog offers additional utility and synergy with the deck's overall strategy.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar, supported by artifacts like Ichor Wellspring and Thoughtcast to generate card advantage. It also includes burn spells like Galvanic Blast to help finish off opponents quickly. The presence of artifacts like Seat of the Synod and Drossforge Bridge enable powerful synergies with the Affinity mechanic, allowing for explosive starts and overwhelming board presence. Nihil Spellbomb provides graveyard hate and disruption against opposing strategies, while cards like Krark-Clan Shaman offer additional removal options. Overall, the deck aims to swarm the opponent with a mix of creatures and burn spells for a fast and aggressive playstyle.

This Gruul Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with efficient creatures like Arbor Elf and Bannerhide Krushok. The deck aims to ramp up quickly with cards like Utopia Sprawl and Wild Growth to play out big threats like Nyxborn Hydra and Eldrazi Repurposer. Jewel Thief provides some card advantage while Writhing Chrysalis and Avenging Hunter offer removal options. The goal is to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain it throughout the match, utilizing the synergy between creatures and spells to push for victory.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells. The deck combines low-cost threats like Krark-Clan Shaman and Gixian Infiltrator with powerful support cards like Refurbished Familiar and Ichor Wellspring. The goal is to control the board with cards like Cast Down and Cleansing Wildfire while maintaining pressure with creatures like Sagu Wildling and Writhing Chrysalis. The deck also incorporates some graveyard interaction with Nihil Spellbomb and Pulse of Murasa. With a balanced mana base and versatile sideboard options, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in fast-paced Pauper 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using a mix of creatures and burn spells. Key cards include Voldaren Epicure, Sneaky Snacker, and Kessig Flamebreather for creature aggression, along with Lightning Bolt, Fiery Temper, and other burn spells to finish off the opponent. The deck aims to apply constant pressure and force the opponent to race against the clock, taking advantage of its low mana curve and efficient spells to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this Rakdos Aggro deck is to aggressively control the board and deal damage quickly to the opponent. The deck focuses on low-cost creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker, as well as burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and Fiery Temper. Additionally, it includes cards like Vampire's Kiss and Alms of the Vein to gain life and maintain pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of powerful creatures and various spells. By utilizing the Urzatron lands to ramp up mana quickly, the player is able to cast big creatures like Avenging Hunter and Bramble Wurm. The deck also includes cards like Relic of Progenitus and Crop Rotation for disruption and flexibility. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats while managing the board through control spells and land manipulation.
